title: Fix mkdirSync static import and verify gates
description: Confirm mkdirSync is statically imported from "fs" at top of src/store/graph.ts, save() has no await, then run npm run build, npm test, and npm run lint to verify all gates pass. Update CLAUDE.md known-issues entry once gates are green.

plan.objective: Verify that src/store/graph.ts uses a static top-level mkdirSync import with no await in save(), run tsc/vitest/eslint gates to exit 0, and remove any stale CLAUDE.md known-issue entry for CART-B01.
key_changes: Audit src/store/graph.ts: confirm mkdirSync is in the single static import { readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync } from 'fs' line
Confirm save() is synchronous — no await keyword, mkdirSync called before writeFileSync
Apply correction to graph.ts only if static-import audit finds a violation (merge mkdirSync into existing import, remove dynamic import and async qualifier)
Remove CART-B01 known-issue bullet from CLAUDE.md ## Known issues section if present
Run npm run build (tsc) — must exit 0 with zero TypeScript errors
Run npm test (vitest run) — must exit 0; CART-B01 regression guard in graph.test.ts must pass
Run npm run lint (eslint src) — must exit 0 with zero violations
No data model changes — Graph, Node, Edge types and graph.json schema are untouched
No new tests required — regression guard already exists in src/store/graph.test.ts

review_plan.objective: Assessed the plan to verify graph.ts uses a static mkdirSync import with a synchronous save(), run the tsc/vitest/eslint gates, and clear any stale CART-B01 entry from CLAUDE.md.
key_changes: Verified independently: graph.ts line 2 has the single combined static import including mkdirSync — no await import('fs') anywhere.
save() is plain synchronous; grep for await/async across the file returns nothing; mkdirSync is called before writeFileSync.
Regression guard exists in graph.test.ts asserting mkdirSync was called and its invocationCallOrder precedes writeFileSync.
CLAUDE.md Known issues section has no CART-B01/mkdirSync bullet; plan Step 2 is a no-op, which the plan correctly anticipates.
No data-model or public-API change; Graph/Node/Edge types and graph.json schema untouched — consistent with offline-only design.
Three-gate suite (build/test/lint) appropriately covers compile, behavior, and lint; testing relies on existing guard rather than redundant tests.
Advisory: acceptance criterion hard-codes 31 tests — implementation should report the actual vitest count instead.
findings: graph.ts static mkdirSync import confirmed; save() synchronous; regression guard present; CLAUDE.md clean; no model change.

approve.objective: Architectural sign-off on the CART-S01-T01 fix verifying graph.ts uses a static mkdirSync import with a synchronous save(), all three gates green, and CLAUDE.md clear of the stale CART-B01 entry.
key_changes: graph.ts line 2 is a single static import including mkdirSync — no dynamic await import('fs'), no async/await in the file.
save() is synchronous; mkdirSync(dir,{recursive:true}) is called before writeFileSync — matches the CART-B01 regression-guard ordering.
No data-model change: Graph/Node/Edge types and graph.json schema untouched; public API surface unchanged.
Consistent with architecture/stack.md ESM, Node-20+, offline-only custom-JSON persistence posture.
No operational impact: no data-loss, breaking-change, performance, or surface-change category applies.
All three gates green per validation: tsc exit 0, vitest 31/31, eslint exit 0; regression guard passing.
Follow-up (future sprint): no concurrency safety on the load()/save() read-modify-write cycle — worth a dedicated task.
